questions.question: Define an operating system. Explain the User View and System View of an operating system. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Definition: A program that manages hardware and acts as intermediary between users and hardware
User View: Ease of use, performance, designed for the way users interact with the system
System View: Resource allocator managing CPU, memory, I/O; control program preventing errors
The kernel is the one program always running on the computer

questions.question: Explain the basic computer system organization with respect to interrupt handling. (3 marks)
keyPoints: CPU and device controllers connected through common bus to shared memory
Device controller informs CPU of completion via interrupt
Interrupt vector table maps each interrupt to its handler; CPU state is saved and restored

questions.question: What is DMA? How does it improve I/O performance? (3 marks)
keyPoints: DMA allows device controllers to transfer blocks directly between buffer and main memory
CPU is not involved in data transfer, only initiates and gets notified on completion
One interrupt per block instead of one per byte, dramatically reducing CPU overhead

questions.question: Compare multiprogramming and time-sharing operating systems. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Multiprogramming: Multiple jobs in memory, CPU switches when current job waits for I/O
Time-sharing: CPU switches rapidly using timer, enabling interactive user sessions
Multiprogramming maximizes CPU utilization; time-sharing minimizes response time
Time-sharing requires process scheduling, virtual memory, and synchronization mechanisms
Both improve system efficiency compared to single-program execution
